name: Russia (Saint Andrew's Ensign)

colors.symbolism: the cross of Saint Andrew, patron saint of Russia and of sailors

description: A white field with a blue saltire (the cross of Saint Andrew) extending corner to corner.
history: Originally introduced by Peter the Great as the ensign of the Imperial Russian Navy from around 1712; abolished after the 1917 Revolution and replaced by Soviet naval ensigns; restored by presidential decree on 26 July 1992 as the official ensign of the Russian Navy.

features.symbolism: the X-shaped cross of Saint Andrew the Apostle, patron saint of Russia
